key_insight: Deep learning on mid-trimester cervical ultrasound showed moderate internal prediction of spontaneous preterm birth, but performance dropped substantially in external validation.
clinical_relevance: The work suggests ultrasound-based AI may help risk stratification, but current models are not yet reliable enough for standalone clinical use.
method: Prospective cohort modeling on the GARBH-Ini dataset
Compared local texture, clinical-variable, deep-learning, and multimodal approaches
Externally validated the best models on an independent cohort scanned on a different ultrasound machine
evidence_signal: Best model achieved AUC 0.71 internally but only 0.52 externally, with wider uncertainty due to few cases.
watch_item: External generalizability was limited, likely due to preterm birth heterogeneity and dataset/machine differences.
paper_title: Development and external validation of deep learning models for spontaneous preterm birth prediction from mid-trimester cervical ultrasound
